Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Every Single Board Computer I Tested in 2025 25 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Microscopes Can See Video on a LaserDisc 37 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. FrameBook 296 upvotes, 56 comments
  4. Pushing and Pulling: Three reactivity algorithms 28 upvotes, 2 comments
  5. LibreOffice Writer now supports Markdown 200 upvotes, 40 comments
  6. Z80 Sans – a disassembler in a font (2024) 43 upvotes, 5 comments
  7. What if the Apple ][ had run on Field-Sequential? 64 upvotes, 12 comments
  8. Artificial-life: A simple (300 lines of code) reproduction of Computational Life 11 upvotes, 0 comments
  9. I made a programming language with M&Ms 21 upvotes, 2 comments
  10. Agent Safehouse – macOS-native sandboxing for local agents 55 upvotes, 13 comments
  11. Neural Boids 69 upvotes, 22 comments
  12. LLM Writing Tropes.md 298 upvotes, 134 comments
  13. Notes on writing Rust-based Wasm 181 upvotes, 78 comments
  14. Log messages are mostly for the people operating your software 78 upvotes, 29 comments
  15. She called 911 for an ambulance. She got a nightmare instead 25 upvotes, 7 comments
  16. We should revisit literate programming in the agent era 19 upvotes, 5 comments
  17. The changing goalposts of AGI and timelines 300 upvotes, 170 comments
  18. Beagle, a source code management system that stores AST trees 71 upvotes, 34 comments
  19. Ask HN: Please restrict new accounts from posting 242 upvotes, 163 comments
  20. WSL Manager 48 upvotes, 25 comments
  21. Lil Finder Guy 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  22. Show HN: I built a real-time OSINT dashboard pulling 15 live global feeds 66 upvotes, 43 comments
  23. The human.json Protocol 18 upvotes, 8 comments
  24. Why can't you tune your guitar? (2019) 139 upvotes, 110 comments
  25. Ask HN: How to be alone? 134 upvotes, 120 comments
  26. Living human brain cells play DOOM on a CL1 [video] 103 upvotes, 91 comments
  27. My “grand vision” for Rust 80 upvotes, 71 comments
  28. My Homelab Setup 78 upvotes, 76 comments
  29. Show HN: Skir – like Protocol Buffer but better 40 upvotes, 35 comments
  30. A basket of new fruit varieties is coming your way 28 upvotes, 24 comments
